name: Apple Pie in Cheddar Crust
recipeIngredient: 7 cups Apples (tart cooking apples such as Mac or Granny Smith)
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the flour, cheese, and salt in a bowl. Using a pastry blender or two knives, cut in the shortening until coarse crumbs form.
2. Sprinkle with iced water (about 5-6 tablespoons) and toss the crumbs until a dough is formed.
3. Press the dough firmly into a ball.
4. Divide the dough almost in half and roll out the larger half on a lightly floured surface to a 13-inch circle.
5. Line a 9-inch pie plate with the pastry, trimming the edges to about 1/2-inch beyond the rim of the pie plate.
6. Combine the apples, sugar, flour, and cinnamon in a bowl, mixing well. Arrange the apple mixture in the lined pie plate.
7. Roll out the remaining dough to an 11-inch circle.
8. Fold into quarters and cut steam slits in the folds. Unfold the crust and place on top of the filling.
9. Fold the top crust under the lower one and flute to form a ridge around the edge of the pie plate.
10. Beat the egg yolk with water, then brush over the top crust and rim.
11. Bake at 400°F for 45-50 minutes or until apples are tender and the crust is golden brown.
12. Cool on a wire rack until slightly warm and serve with vanilla ice cream, if desired.
